commit.message:
feat: Turnstile CAPTCHA, persistent rate limiting, and Azure Queue for feedback (#45)

* feat: add Turnstile CAPTCHA, persistent rate limiting, and queue-based issue creation

- Add Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA to feedback form (frontend widget +
  server-side token verification via TurnstileVerifier); skipped when
  TURNSTILE_SECRET is not configured (dev/CI mode)
- Replace in-memory token bucket with Azure Table Storage (TableStorageRateLimiter)
  so limits persist across cold starts and are safe under concurrent scale-out
  via ETag-based optimistic concurrency
- Add global daily submission cap (DailyCounter) backed by Azure Table Storage;
  configurable via DAILY_SUBMISSION_LIMIT app setting (default 100)
- Decouple GitHub issue creation from form submission using Azure Storage Queue:
  SubmitFunction validates, rate-limits, and enqueues; new ProcessQueueFunction
  creates the GitHub issue asynchronously with built-in retry/dead-letter support
- Update Feedback.csproj with Azure.Data.Tables and Storage.Queues extensions
- Update local.settings.json.example, site/.env.example, and infra/provision.sh
  with new settings (TURNSTILE_SECRET set manually like GITHUB_TOKEN)

Closes #44

Co-authored-by: Sunny Kolattukudy <kolatts@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: inject PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY into pages-deploy workflow

* feat: SendGrid email notifications and queue-based submission processing

- Add email field to feedback form with localStorage pre-fill persistence
- Store pending submissions in Azure Table Storage (PendingSubmissions table)
  instead of processing immediately; timer runs every 10 minutes to promote
  pending submissions to GitHub issues up to the daily cap (default 10)
- Send SendGrid confirmation email to submitter after issue is created
- Add POST /api/webhook/issue endpoint authenticated via GitHub HMAC-SHA256
  signature; sends closed-notification email when a from-website issue is closed
- Store issue→email mappings in IssueEmails table for closed notifications
- Replace token-bucket rate limiter with configurable per-IP daily limit
  (IP_DAILY_LIMIT, default 1); swap queue extension for timer extension
- Document SENDGRID_API_KEY, WEBHOOK_API_KEY, and GitHub webhook setup in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: carry over unprocessed submissions across days

GetAllPendingAsync uses a 7-day OData range query so submissions not
processed due to hitting the daily cap are retried on subsequent days,
ordered oldest-first to prevent starvation. Daily cap still counts only
today's processed submissions via GetAllTodayAsync.

Update provision.sh, README, and local.settings.json.example accordingly.

Fix reconnect to not start duplicate scan process

- Changed reconnect logic to NOT call /scan endpoint
- Calling /scan would start a new run_pipeline.py subprocess

- Poll /api/scans/<repo> every 5 seconds for completion status
- Prevents duplicate experiments from being kicked off
- Shows 'Scan in progress' message while waiting
- Notifies user when scan completes with elapsed time

Fixed issue where page refresh + repo selection would create new exp.

Fix led.osc: use blocking read_byte instead of read_nonblock

Switched to read_byte(0) which blocks until input arrives —
simpler, more reliable, and matches the C reference (led.c).

commit.message:
fix: scale text watermark font_size and margin proportionally to output width

font_size and margin are stored in native-video-pixel units. The UI preview
correctly scales them via scaleFactor = containerWidth / videoNaturalWidth.
The backend was applying them as absolute pixels regardless of the output
resolution, so on a 640px GIF from a 1920px video a 100px font rendered at
100/640 = 15.6% of the GIF width instead of the intended 100/1920 = 5.2%.

Fix: in build_video_filters, compute
  scale = effective_video_width / native_video_width
and apply it to both font_size and margin (text) and margin (image overlay)
before building the drawtext / overlay filter string.

Thread native_video_width through the entire call stack:
- build_video_filters(opts, mid_filters, effective, native)
- build_gif_filters(opts, fps, gif_width, native)
- extract_frame(input, ts, out, opts, effective, native)
- extract_frames(..., effective, native)
- generate_gif(..., native)
- gif.rs: reads session.width as native_video_width
- export.rs / frame.rs: already read session.width; pass as native

New unit tests added:
- build_video_filters_text_watermark_scales_for_smaller_output
  (effective=640, native=1920: fontsize=100→33, margin=30→10)
- build_gif_filters_text_watermark_scales_font
  (gif=640, native=1920: fontsize=96→32)

All 38 tests pass; 2 ignored integration tests confirmed green.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: landing page — pure CSS rewrite, zero Tailwind dependency

Root cause: project has no tailwindcss installed. All Tailwind classes
in JSX rendered unstyled. Previous nav fix was correct approach.

This commit extends it to the entire landing page:
- All styles moved to LANDING_CSS const as raw .lp-* classes
- Media queries and :hover in CSS, not className
- Zero Tailwind utility classes in JSX
- 11 sections: hero, social proof, ROI, flow, decisions,
  portal, case study, pricing, CTA, footer
- Premium communication: outcomes > features, ROI section
  with 73x callout, decision log as killer feature
